text: Tmobile rippoff I've been with them for 14 yrs. They will charge you for an old line you don't have anymore when you get a new phone or tablet if it has a different number tell them to cancel old line wait 6months then they will still charge you for cancelled line rippoff, scam rippoff I'm having friends and family get rid of tmobile

T-mobile Gulfgate in Houston has VERY POOR SERVICE REPS.They will wreck your new screen protector & NOT TAKE RESPONSIBILITY for it.I took it back to the store the next morning (didn't discover till 3am).They wouldn't replace it.They're ALL MEXICAN & I'm WHITE.Everytime I go there I'm the ONLY WHITE person there.They also sold me a phone without my looking it over (everything). I'm also a Senior Citizen.The Security Guard kept letting a baby scream. Gulfgate has a rating of 3.9 which should be 1.0.

T Mobile and Asurion have defrauded me out of $400.  In October of last year, I used my insurance to replace my phone.  Despite returning my phone in the prepaid envelope sent to me for that purpose, T Mobile took $400 from my account.  Since then, I have spent hours on the phone with both T Mobile and Asurion only to be told repeatedly they will not return my money because, although they have record of receiving my package, the contents of the package were not noted by Asurion's warehouse.  T Mobile stole $400 from my account due to the faulty record keeping of Asurion.  I have plans to take them to small claims court.

Let me start by saying I love T mobile and have been a long time customer.
I just left the store on Mudd Lane in Louisville where I encountered the rudest T-Mobile employee I have ever interacted with.  Ana could not understand why I would not want to pay the $20.00 warrantee fee on a phone that was 2 weeks old.  It was not the $20.00 that I take issue with, it is the fact that I would expect a phone to work well with normal use after just 2 weeks. I suggested that there must be a way to wave the fee and it was not until I insisted on a manger find a way to make this right that I was told the fee could be applied as a credit, a perfectly reasonable compromise.  When i asked for a receipt stating a new phone would be ordered and a credit would be applied to my account Ana was once again rude and acted as if a receipt is not something that would ever be standard issue?
Come on T-mobile, you are so much better than this.  Surely this is not how you want to be represented.

Absolutely do not use t-mobile, very very poor coverage, couldn't even talk to my next door neighbor without dropping my call. Talked to t-mobile about the problem and they agreed there is a coverage problem. Since I need my phone for work, I  changed to Verizon with no problem for coverage. T-mobil advised that I would have to pay for the remaining time on the contract over $1000, I sent in a $200 payment and I  was told by T-mobile I could pay any amount I wanted, next thing I knew, T-Mobile turned my into a collection agency. Stay away, cheaper is not always better.

Just want to sincerely express appreciation for representative Leslie (9948600) at Clive IA location. She was so incredibly kind,thorough and patient with a "nontechie" customer like me. She made me feel my phone switchover and setup was her priority. What a sweetheart!

If I could give less stars to this company I would!!! Bought a phone from T mobile, unable to call or text anyone, always says no service!!! Decided to cancel, and the final straw came from this useless company. The final bill amounts to 17 cents, which we would need to pay online through their payment portal!!! but guess what, the minimum amount to input is 5 fricking dollars!!! The only other mode of payment would be to call customer service and be charged an extra $10!!! on a 17 cent bill!!! screw this garbage company, choose boost mobile or some other service

I had been with metro psc for over ten years Never had a problem.
Now that they are t-mobile; it's been a constant drag. My internet connection is always slow, I get kicked off my games, my texts, pictures, emails, struggle to get through.
I haven't moved, or changed anything, it's them. I'm not getting what I've paid for.
I paid for metro psc.
I didn't switch.
They did.
If I wanted t-mobile, I would have sought them out myself.
I am so very unhappy with my service, I will be switching.
They're practically thieves.

When I first signed up for service, everything was great.

A few months later and their service is terrible. Can't make calls out and can't hear the person on the other end.

They blame it on the towers in Thousand Oaks CA.

What kind of company cannot fix their own towers to provide at least decent reception.

Already have other companies with better ratings lined up.

Adios T Mobile. You Truly Suck!!

We purchased Samsung S20plus,Samsung 21Ultra,11pro max IPhone,12 pro max, plus 2 more 12pro max.  I have sent back the S20plus because they told me that it needed a mother board and I would have to send it off to get fixed.  So we got s21 ultra, and those phones kept going back to reset, then I have sent back 7 sets of Iphone 12 pro max and they keep going back to reset.  Been to the apple store 4 times, geek squad, ibreak you fix, and still going back to reset.  Been talking with 10 tier 2 supv with apple.  Nobody knows what is going on. I am fed up and going to go with another carrier. Was told that someone was physically going into our phone and resetting it.  Really.  We have our phones on us all the time.

Stop by T-mobile at the Form in San Antonio,TX, there are two store at this location, asked about the purchase of a flip phone not at one location was then referred to the 2nd location all she could say was I can show you a picture. I was try to purchase a new phone not buy a picture. I was with sprint for over 15 years and with it now becoming T-mobile, I, find myself looking for another carrier

Found a phone on Ebay, did the IMEI check at T-Mobile and was told it's perfect for my location. Bought the phone. Bought the SIM card.
Tried multiple times to activate it online, several times with the help of "Customer Care"...but not Pre-Paid Customer Care because there isn't one. Just the Customer Care that "can't do that".
Every time I went to pay at checkout, the system took my money, displayed a message that I "Need to show an ID to store employee", then refunded my money immediately. So we drive 100 miles RT to the nearest store. There I was looked at as a crazy person because Mr. 12 Years Employed by TM had never heard of such an error message, nor had anyone else. So Mr. 12 Years activate my phone instantly, with no ID required. The phone number was for the current location, so I requested another in my area. No problem. Got a good one. I quickly made a call to make sure it worked and we left. 
Upon arrival at home, I tried to make a call. No service. No bars. I got back on "Customer Care" (on a PC people!-shocking how many times I had to say that) and after several hours I found a guy who knew the issue. My T-Mobile phone, running on T-Mobile service would not work in my location because of....wait for it.....the phone didn't support the BANDS used by T-Mobile.Not even outside. (I can almost see the tower from the road).
Back to Customer Care for a refund. Another two hours on the old Tracfone flip phone got the job done. Unsure as to whether I will actually get the refund. I'm not holding my breath.
All of this took about two weeks, at the tail-end of a nearly 2 month ordeal. 
 
So, F U T-Mobile. My first experience with you will also be my last.

TMobile is the worst company ever! Look at your bills people you are being billed an extra 2 weeks of service! Customer Service is so rude! When you sign up they tell you what amount you will pay which is a lie! You are basically paying for overpriced prepaid service better off if you go to metro for half the price!

Been with TMobile for less than 2 month they have already pulled of my banc account over $600 dollars.... when I signed up for tm I was told $70 for the service $19 for equipment and 19 for equipment insurance... ( crazy hidden fees ) now I was going to pay my bill they stated My bill is $136 and change.... ahh what in the FCK is going on here???? The worst customer service, phone service sucks because I don’t have signal... do not !!! Use Tmobile .... I wouldn’t recommend it at all ...

I had Sprint up until T-mobile took over. I need my phone to do my job. I do a lot of stuff outside that requires good service to connect my phone to my computer. Had absolutely no issue with Sprint. It was always fast and reliable. Today it took over 20 minutes for some photos I took to even show up on my computer. With Sprint it was instantaneous. I will be switching providers tonight. I had heard nothing but bad things about T mobile before the switch so I was weary going into this. I now know the full reasons for the scrutiny and I could not agree more with it.

I went to t mobile to switch carriers and up grade phones. I have great credit and for 2 new phones and a tablet they wanted $1,000 dollar down payment because I don't have credit with t mobile. I would never recommend anyone to become new customers.

I had been a long time customer with tmobile and had the worst experience and treated like nobody at the Cleveland tn branch, so I went to another carrier. My final bill I received last week and 2 days later a collection notice. I have been in contact with them and talked to them prior to final bill which no one could tell me. Ohhhh it's an internal Collection he says its no big deal. Yeah right not the point. Service is so spotty around here on top of not training sales associates. I do not recommend them at all.

If I could give this less than a 1 star review I would. I used to have T-Mobile in the past and I couldn't be more aggravated that they took over Sprint. I have Sprint and I WANT Sprint back. Shove your money up your rear end. It was the only service provider I actually liked and you just run them into the ground. You took all their towers, right? So why in the Almighty's name are you messing with my cell phone? I don't get service. Anywhere. How? It drops calls. It won't let me even call my voicemail. It says I have bars. What is wrong with you people? I will NOT be staying with T-Mobile. You should be ashamed of yourselves and your wretched company. Give me back my damn Sprint towers and service.

T mobile, is the biggest con game in the phone business.  I PAID my bill, and cancelled service with them.  NOW....they want $68 for 2 WEEKS of what they call (outstanding) billing.  BULL puckey
!   I CLEARED MY ACCOUNT before changing to another company! BTW, I was with them for 16 years! Unheard of in the cell business!

After installing a new T-Mobile SIM card in my Sprint phone, I had extremely poor service in one particular very rural area near McHenry, Maryland. After spending at least one hour on-line chatting via WiFi with a Sprint representative in an attempt to improve my service, I lost all service for several days. Then, while traveling for a funeral, I took my phone to a T-Mobile store in Windsor, CT, where the store manager, Abigail Surrell, restored my service in about 10 minutes. Now it's better than every. Thank you Abigail. Of course, I have no praise for the original on-line chat representative from Sprint!

T-Mobile on Howard Road is fantastic manager and Associates are wonderful they are a great help their patients they take time with you to understand if you're buying a new phone they're awesome definitely recommend to purchase phones from T-Mobile on Howard Road in Rochester New York

There service is bad.  I cannot get calls to come through and only get voice-mail after someone has called me.  This could cost me jobs which in turn cost me money to raise my family.  Real glad big tech companies can offer crappy service and charge outrageous prices for it.

I cancelled my account with T- Mobile 2 months ago because the service was terrible. My husband with Canadian phone was getting better reception in US than I!
However, after 2 months of cancellation they are still charging me on my credit card. When I called them to solve the issue they asked for a password on the account, but I never had a password!!!! So they said they can't get to my account and they can't give my money back. So basically they are stealing my money and refuse to get to my account to fix the problem.

Terrible customer service including Shannell emp ID 1212931 and shamika. Unprofessional and don't appologize for their mistakes. Kept me on the phone for 2 hours and over charged me for things I didn't use.

They told us that we were only going to pay for service but in reality your paying at least 150 dolllars more than they say. The representatives are part of a scam. They make you think you have to pay nothing until you get the phones. T mobile is a scam. DO NOT TRUST ANYTHING THEY SAY.

I needed faster speed for my 5G phone.  I know performance is a matter of device/technology/coverage near my area

I had tried AT&T prepaid, Cricket, Visible, all on the largest/fastest/mostest-whatever unlimited service...

My service speed and quality deteriorated with each in the order mentioned above!

I decided to try T-Mobile prepaid...  On the speed, they delivered!  A sweet 200 Mbps in my area!

Within the same week, I went back to my local T-Mobile store to switch from prepaid to postpaid, so I can explore and benefit from some of the additional perks on postpaid.  I activated 3 lines on postpaid Magenta Max.

On the cost includes all fees and taxes, they delivered!

On the speed on postpaid, they delivered!

On the perks with the Tuesdays app...  I'm still waiting to find something that appeals me enough to go get it, but this is far from a dealbreaker!

On the additional add-ons (included in Magenta Max), they delivered!  The SCAM Shield works good.  The DIGITIS app works good and with that, an additional phone number provided to preserve additional confidentiality when dialing out.  All that included in my Magenta Max monthly plan, in addition to unlimited text, talk and data!  On all this, they delivered!

On service, over the phone, I wish I had written down the name of the person I spoke with to provide specific kudos to that person!  First, yes, service is over the phone, not self-service via a web page, you actually speak with a human!  She handled my questions and responded with clear information that was all accurate.  She ended the call by saying she was going on vacation but she would contact me once she had returned...  Me, based on all my previous customer service experience, I had forgot about this, assuming I would never get a call back...  But guess what?  Exactly on the day she had said to be back, the phone rings and there she was, following-up on my request!  I had a remaining question and she committed to address it herself directly, so I don't have to go through repeating my story again, and that she would call me back in about 3 days on this!  On customer service, not only they delivered but exceeded my expectations!

From my local T-Mobile, I also received the same experience, very courteous, speedy and clear explanations.  On the locally provided service, T-Mobile delivered as well!

I do not receive compensation for my review and I am not affiliated with T-Mobile in any ways.  I am just a happy customer!

I do not understand the Sprint lease, never explained to me, impossible to own a phone, three different answers from tha same company... piece of... never ever dealing with them again. Horrible business,  never ever hace an account with them

GARBAGE, been calling about the same issue for 4 days and it has not been resolved and then i was told that they changed my service from 5g to 3g, after using less that 7 gigs of data. AVOID AVOID AVOID AVOID!!!!
I have been there twice and it’s been great experience with this young guy very professional Rafael Tapia.I am really happy very helpful, best customer service.I will recommend my friends T-Mobile

WOW...Courtney Silas who is the Store Manager and Glenn Ordway.
Talk about unbelievable knowledge and service. I went to the Oakleaf store with a slew of questions because I am coming over from Sprint, and because of that I had a few hiccups due to the merger not at either Agents Fault.  But to no avail they jumped into action with a solution that works out for me in the end. I can't believe how informative and welcoming these 2 were and if you ever are in the area please be sure to ask for either one of them, they make you feel like Family which is important in this time and age. I am THRILLED to say I am a forever T-Mobile customer...  Thank You again Courtney and Glenn..

Respectfully,
Alyse V.

I recently switched my Sprint Sim card to a T-mobile sim card. The data and cell coverage are horrendous. I got a plan for cheap but would never recommend anyone to use t-mobile in the greater Denver metro area.
I am certain there in an I in mobile.  Bloody yanks need to learn English.

Just switched to Verizon last night. T-Moble sucks. I called them last night and it took 1 hour and 10 minutes before I was able to speak to someone. I will never go back to their dropped calls, poor customer service and unable to send texts that happens frequently.

I had the worst customer service experience of my life with T-Mobile. I am too frustrated at this point where they are making me go in circles and setting wrong expectations again and again. Their managers are too good to come on the phone and tackle the issue themselves (as they are always out of the office). I am in a different country and can’t use my phone because they are unable to unlock my sim (the device is fully paid). Every rep at T-Mobile I am talking to is giving me different and wrong information. It’s been more than 96 hours and I am still juggling between the calls with their uneducated representatives. Can’t wait to come back to the US and change my carrier once and for all. I do not recommend T-Mobile please don’t waste your time and money on it. Verizon is the best.
T Mobile service is horrible. I have 5G and my phone runs slower than a snail. I really hate their service. 🤬🤬🤬✌🏾
when we tried to leave the company, they charged is with a $559 dollar bill. found out we were overpaying and didn't do their jobs properly so now we had to pay for it. STEER CLEAR OF T MOBILE!

If you had negative stars, T-mobile would earn FIVE of them!

I have been a Sprint customer even before Sprint got into the mobile business, something like 20 years or so. I stayed with Sprint because they ALWAYS took care of the customer. Then the WORST possible thing happened, T-mobile bought Sprint.

A few months ago I decided to add another line to the account. BIG MISTAKE.  Was told it would be added to my CURRENT plan. BIG LIE. After SIX HOURS (yes, SIX HOURS) on the phone I still did NOT have service on the new line. FINALLY, I got transferred to an original Sprint employee and FIVE MINUTES (yes, FIVE MINUTES) later had service.

I get my bill. OUCH! The line was NOT added to my current plan. Instead, I was being charged $70 plus fees just for the new line which happens to be MORE than the TWO LINES on my CURRENT plan are costing! Turns out that I was LIED to. The ONLY option was keeping the line and paying $25 per month plus fees for the new line. NOT A HAPPY CAMPER.

Last month voice mail was changed. Sprint voicemail worked fine.  So T-mobile "IMPROVED" voicemail and now it is CRAP!

First, I CAN NO LONGER reliably SKIP calls in voicemail - sometimes they skip, sometimes they get saved, and sometimes they get erased.

Second, there is no longer a phone number and time stamp at the beginning of calls.  That means I must LISTEN to every call (using more minutes) instead of skipping to the call I want to hear. And if the caller does not give his phone number (and it does not show in my missed calls), I have NO WAY TO RETURN HIS CALLS and NO reliable way to skip.

Third, the voice message announcer must be talking with marbles in her mouth because her enunciation is almost INCOMPREHENSIBLE!  NEVER was a problem with Sprint as their announcer was ALWAYS understandable.

Bottom line is that my voice messages are damn hard to use, assuming that they don't vaporize in the process.

My opinion? T-MOBILE SUCKS BIG TIME. I would NOT wish this company on my WORST enemy!

I have been a t mobile customer for 7 years. I am happy with customer service and there prices. What I am not happy with is that someone was able to add a iphone to my account on 3/6/2021. And again on 5/4/2021 because the person that the store did not follow the added instruction for my account because i am a victim of identity theft. I am supposed to be the only one who can make changes to my account. As much as i like them i will be leaving them if this happens one more time.

Their service is horrible. I am in Arizona and I can't make calls, or calls drop, or either I can't hear the person on the other line or they can't here me. The worse is when I actually am able to make a call and the phone completely shuts down and reboots. After 5 months of trying to work things out with them there has been no resolution.  No contract but in reality there is because if you want to leave then the you are responsible for paying off the phone they said was free. For me they said its $610.  I have had them tell me its a bad phone,changed phone. Same problem. Changed sim card, same problem.  This company is a disgrace and abomination. Beware. I would have rated in the negative side if that was an option.

I can't take it anymore. I thought sprint was bad before the merger, but now it is worse than ever. I can't even talk to anyone on the phone now, much less send text or access the internet. I can't even be sure that this complaint will send. It's that bad. It's been about two weeks since I have been able to have an entire conversation with someone on the phone. After a few words its gone. And worse still, customer service could care less. Worse than that, there is nothing that can be done about it. As far as I'm concerned, I should receive a refund for the past few months of the non-existent service.

Called from landline regarding cell phone activation. Robot said wait time was 45 min,or ask for call back, I did . I waited 1 hour and 15 mins, no call. I called again and waited 1 hour and 10 min on line. Maria answered ,poor phone connection "I can't hear yo,have a nice day". no one else has had problems hearing me. I am now waiting 1 hour and 30 mins for a call back. POOR POOR

I don't like companies that outsource their call center taking jobs away from Americans. That being said I had to change plans and three different people that I talked to were  EXTREMELY hard to understand what they were saying! Ridiculous!!!

I have been a customer of Sprint for many many years with no problems until they merged with T-Mobile.  The service sucks.  I do not have service in many places.  I cannot even use my phone in my own home.  T-Mobile suggested I get their SIM card put it.  That was an even worse mistake.  After the SIM card was put in, I did not believe the service would be worse, but it is.  After 20 years with Sprint I am now a happy customer o Verizon and get service everywhere.  Do not use T-Mobile.  They are the worst!!!

We've been with T-Mobile for 8+ years now, by necessity, as they are the only ones who provide service at our location (despite the fact that we live in big city). Both T-Mobile's coverage and customer service continue to vastly improve. It used to be somewhat frustrating, but their customer service is much faster and more helpful than it used to be. My billing issues were resolved by phone within 20 minutes (including time to connect to a rep), kindly and to our satisfaction. Their rollout of 5G is improving our coverage. We have gone from begrudging customers to happy customers, and will continue with them even if other carriers fix their coverage. Another perk is a new store close to our house with by great service & short waits.
They are terrible now that they merged! And when you cancel you have to wait until the end of your billing cycle, what a con!run run run!!!!

My dad just switched to t-mobiles new "5g" two lines for $70 with two "free" phone plans. It sounded great, but they're charging for the phones each month. They're using the word "FREE" in big bold letters, and calling the monthly payments "credits." The person that helped us at the store very dishonestly assured my dad that the phones were completely free, and that he would "receive a credit" for the phones to be free each month. I don't know how this isn't exploiting the elderly. I would expect this kind of stuff from a cash for gold place or a quick n loan, but not t-mobile. Congratulations t-mobile, you're off brand now. If you keep going like this, you're going to become Yahoo.

I wish I could give them a negative number. Been with Sprint since 2003 and looking a new company. I ordered a new phone that the carrier said was delivered but not. Had to file a police report to get another phone. Now rather than overnight, I must wait a week to receive the phone. All this could have been avoided if they required a signature. Took me 3 hours on the phone to resolve. Do not do it. Find a company other than t mobile sprint.

Just stopped by TMobile (Glastonbury, CT) on my way home to replace my headphones. I was helped by two young ladies were super friendly, patient, and made sure I had what I needed before leaving the store. They definitely went above and beyond!

The worst experience ever! Was going to switch from Verizon. Ever since  Contacting T-Mobile it has been a nightmare they charged me for SIM cards that I didn't even get yet got a bill about 4 days later $111 for service that was never even activated have spent so much time on the phone with "experts" so they say,it's been horrible.  Confirmed my account was closed and just got a past due bill!!!! Calling then AGAIN
I pay 75$ a month for unlimited I went a little over and paid and exra 100 for more data and today I get loading speeds of 0.76 mb per second what a joke you guy rob people
Obi at T-mobile in Citrus Park is awesome! He was knowledgeable and kind.  I have been a T-mobile customer for years and I will not consider any other carrier because of employees like Obi!

My husband sign up on line to switch our phones, sim cards came and can not get them to work.  have called numerous times and keep getting the run around.  also signed up for auto pay, keep receiving bills for more than quoted amount, will not help me close account, they just keep billing.  when to the store last week and the people there tried to help but the call center kept hanging up on them also.  the gentlemen there said he would get back to me, but i have not heard from him.  Called him today and he said that no one has returned or gotten back to him.  He did inform me last week to get out of this mess it would cost me $180.00 for a service i have not been able to use!!  This company is a bunch of thiefs

Absolute garbage.  Used to be good, but now customer service sucks, price sucks, service sucks and the experience is just painful!

Avoid.

Tmobile is a greedy company now that they keep buying other companies the loyal customers get the horrible consumer service horrible phone connection they do not have enough towers to support all the phones connections and what i want to is how you offering the new sprint customers free phone because they are a loyal customer really your tmobile customer are the loyal customers not the  ones that were force to become tmobile because one thing i know for sure is that sprint customers did not want to become tmobile customers

I just saw your commercial for having the best 5G service I was with Verizon for 20 years I left them to join T-Mobile to save $30 a month after 5 months I paid $1,600 out of my pocket to break my contract with you T-Mobile who has the worst 5G service ever to go back to Verizon stop your false advertisement your 5G service sucks your phone service sucks your text messaging service sucks there is nothing good about T-Mobile

If I could give 0 stars I would.
Worst customer service, ever!

I wish I could give them no stars! This company is a joke. They lied to us for months and months and then sent us to collections while we were waiting for them to send us a box to return and item because we were told that was the only way to return it. There customer service is horrible. The wait time is insane and they are so beyond rude. They tell you all the time don’t worry don’t worry we will take care of it. Well you should worry if you have t mobile because they will lie to you and tell you what you want to hear just to get off the call! This company should not exist! Then when you talk to a manager they tell you that nothing is in the account notes so they can’t believe what you are telling them. Then you ask for your call to be listened to because they claim they record all calls but you know that’s not possible as they say! If you are thinking about using t mobile think again! You will regret it just like we did!!

I'd give a zero but it makes u add a star... We  long with all of America have struggled horrible thru this pandemic. TMobile is the only company NOT helping people.. my phone continues to get shut off and I catch up just to have them say it's the same yet they are automatically taking payments. This has been the worst service ever. My machine to live is attached to my phone and I'm struggling hard to keep up but am unable to do so and there only option is keep shutting us off... We have less income due to this stupid pandemic. I'm turning this company off as it's been the absolute highest bills and worse customer service ever!!! Even my car loan has greatly goes us thru this pandemic..  tmobil makes so much money and has zero help toong time customers.

I have not had reliable service since I switched from AT&T. Constant connection issues after buying cell booster. My tower is constantly having issues and it is located a few minutes les away. After buying this cell booster my service had not gotten better for calls and data, which is extremely slow. My calls cannot connect to cell service, delayed calls to others, text messages are not received for hours. I am mostly bed bound and need my phone for help if needed and doctors to seek help with telemedicine.

Disgusting
 
There are So DIGUSTING !!! they change my bill EVERY MONTH !!!!! I have to call every month to see why the bill went up !!!! Im so tired of them !!! THIS IS THE WORST CELL COMPANY !!!!!!!! and they dont even have signal anywere

The T-Mobile in Marlow Heights is awesome! I just want to say thank you to Kennedy, Diamond, Kristin for all of their help and understanding! Kennedy welcomed us as soon as we came in the door. Everyone was friendly and professional. Hands down the best service we’ve ever received! We will spread the word to others and we will for sure be back!

Tmobile is the worst business relationship in 53 yrs of my life. They are not trustworthy & will steal from you. I cant even speak to them about my account because they saymy password is not valid & they continue to steal my money. I have to go in a store to get a new password. Do yourself a favor & go elsewhere for cell service. Oh & their coverage is a joke!

If I could I wouldn’t even give 1 star. This company is a complete ripoff, I was a customer for 2 weeks and noticed my bill looking extremely weird as they had added extra payments to my account that I specificly told them I did not want. I had specificly told them how I want 2 lines of the cheapest sevice they have. However, they were going to charge me for 280$ for a month of service for 2 lines which is ridiculous. Do not get anything from t mobile, please.
Disgusting, dishonest, liers, bait and switch.  I give them a -100 for these business practices.....

If you're sprint don't put tmobiles new sim card in...I did and now I can't receive calls and they can't help me when it is there fault...they said I have to send my phone to Samsung..it was a huge mistake on my part I should've known better

They are a total rip off . I originally had 4 lines for $100 and later dropped 2 lines . The price went down after dropping the 2 lines, as it should. The price dropped for two months, then went back up $30 and they had the audacity to tell me that was the original price I should have been be paying when and that the two lines are still $100.
Johnny was great in helping me!!

Went to tmobile to have them put in a new Sim card that THEY recommend I do" nobody knows what they are doing as when I got my phone back EVERTHING WAS MISSING! ALL MY PHOTOS GONE! all my contacts, messages, emails, printer set ups. You name it! All gone! My phone basically a pc of junk! Went to another store where they recovered some files but my phone is CRAP NOW! no recourse other than they told me jeez this never happened before " my old chip worked better than this new one. I don't get service half the time now!
T-mobile socks and they don't care to make anything right

I am not happy with T mobile. Moved over because At & T was adding new charges to my bill and the monthly payment kept increasing, so we moved over to Sprint. Then they changed over to T mobile, and suddenly our phones suspiciously stopped working. The new phones we bought from T mobile stopped working as soon as we got home. They drop calls, ghost call, don't call, and they shut off for no reason. I've been given the run around for months with the insurance company, the phone manufacturer, and T mobile. Everyone tells me something different. I dropped my insurance policy because it's not worth it. It's better to buy your phone over amazon, unless you are an apple user, and then just purchase a plan.
The phones are getting too expensive, but either go look online at best buy, since people return phones they have just bought, and they are priced lower, or buy a blu phone for your teen. Some of these phones start at around $58.00. Don't buy into any contract and don't buy any phone at any carrier. Get some back up less expensive phones online for emergencies where you just change the sym card when your phone stops working. Even better, get some of those senior citizen flip phones for your teen when they are obnoxious and don't obey house rules or disrespect you.

I've had it. I have been with T-Mobile since I first got a cell phone back in 2006. Over the years, I always defended them when everyone else said that "they are a mobile service for peasants". It's becoming clear to me now that those people are right. Time after time, T-Mobile has failed me. Multiple times a day, daily, I'll have full bars (on Wi-Fi & Mobile) and texts will get the spinning wheel and not send. The other day, I got a text, from myself...that I didn't send. People tell me that my side of the line sounds muffled when I talk to them on the phone. I'm in sales and miss business calls because they simply don't come through. I've tried and tried and tried to make this work. These issues really started about 2 years ago. To make things worse, they require $500 upfront for a new phone because they finance "up to $750". I called AT&T and they financed my phone ($1300) and got me a new line for $140 down. After calling T-Mobile to tell them I was leaving - they said that they couldn't do anything. I'm severely disappointed. Beyond frustrated. And will be vocally hating on T-Mobile to everyone that I speak to about phone service from now on. You guys failed me and lost a lifelong and loyal customer. Not that you care.

T Mobile Internet for me has been a bad experience. I was promised 50 mbps speed, but it has averaged 4 to 7. Occasionally, it will actually get to 15 but that is rare. The tower is continuously going to “no service” as well. Forget about their ‘support experts’. They are mostly clueless. And it takes hours to reach them. I sent my Internet box back to them last month but got a 200.00 bill today because they claim they did not receive it. Another dishonest company.
Do customers lose internet connection from T-Mobile in their homes due to low flying commercial aircraft on takeoffs and landings? We seem to.

Their customer support seems to be 100% overseas and they are by far some of the most incompetent people who have ever failed to complete a simple request. Resolving the issue after their mistake, was nearly impossible and every useless frontline person who answered the phone or chat messages pretended they could understand and fix the issue. Sprint customer service was WAY better before acquired by T-Mobile. This companies low rating is well deserved.

I've been with T-Mobile for years now and instead of the service getting better, it's gotten worse. I need my phone to clock in and out for work but I can't get any service what so ever inside the building. Soon as I find a better provider, it's goodbye T-Mobile for good.
no just no omg t-moblie took over my fave phone store

I sent them my Galaxy S8 back in November of 2020 for a discount promotion. I didnt receive the discount at all. Then i called them to ask what happened. They said they found a problem with my device, so now its April 22 2021 why didnt they return my device back to me? why did they keep my device if it was not working? why didnt tell me about it as well? They have NO answer!! WORST phone company out there! and they have the audacity to claim they win awards each year for customer service excellency lol....how when you have 1.5/5 star ratings on all business sites.

Citrus Hills Fl T-Mobile; unprofessional misleading and then make it sound as if it is your fault, the phones do not work in an area which is already known to have very poor reception; yet they tout it as "going to work 5X's better than your old service. "NOT" took the phones (4) back the next day and was charged a restocking fee $89.00 that was not made known because it was on the boxes which we did not see til we got home, Rep. had put them in the bag. Now they're holding my phone numbers from another provider "hostage" Most awful experience, Go elsewhere for your service or stay with your current provider... Cows always think the grass is greener on the other side. T-Mobile should have their Executive Administration, marketing and employees trained by watching Miracle on 34th Street (Christmas movie) to start with and a test as to what they learned afterwards. If T_MOBILE CANNOT service a customer suggest they stay with their provider or go elsewhere! "More BEES WITH HONEY" ***UPDATE*** Just received a bill from a collection agency for T-Mobile for $122.16 the cell phones and contract were returned and cancelled the very next day! This company, is not worthy of any customers!

They auto paid themselves after I canceled my device plan without a statement given to me. No statement in the mail, email or in the store. The store manager said there is no statement made for that month that they charged me!! This is unlawful! I am going through the FTC to get my money back

Best advice is go somewhere else, will NEVER have their service again, ever. Will be following up with a complaint with the BBB. Around the same time my tablet was acting up and could not use it I received not a notice but a final bill for a much larger amount than usual, wasted more time trying to straightening that out, and the 2 separate numbers that went to my tablets were given away to someone else and they wanted $20 for 2 new SIM cards for new numbers. A week ago my tablets are not working again, I texted the numbers and they did not work, today I got a final notice for a final bill of $250 5 x higher than my normal monthly bill, and last month I overpaid. Totally done and I will return the new tablet I got and they can shove it up their ass and no more, horrible service, customer service is worse
I was able to get help, and it was the best services ever. All my question and concern were address. The services couldn't have been better. It was in WBP location.
5G was extremely disappointing

T mobile has terrible 5g yet they advertise as being soooo good. All it does is lag, works slow, and constantly makes you turn off mobile data and back on just to get internet to start working for a few more seconds. They need to figure it out.

I am a senior citizen who does not use her phone often. I was switching to a new phone (my same old plan). A young man named Valent at Woodmere Shopping Center in Silver Spring, MD was extremely helpful and patient. He did everything I asked and gave good explanations. Thanks, Valent.

I was really hopeful of having Tmobile home internet. I had problems with it the very first day and never even bothered getting rid of my old internet provider because the coverage was bad everywhere in the house. So this unit sat here doing nothing. I didn't get any bills for last couple months. It won't let me sign in with my number. Then I randomly get a cancel notice and return slip for equipment. That's fine with me....send this garbage back. So I call in so see if I have any bills for the internet I couldn't use. One guy said yes but transferred me to someone else that's in the home internet department. I didn't get any new information from him other than to wait two months to call back in and see what the bill is. He said they  must have typed in the wrong billing address because it was different than what my billing address actually is. That's why I didn't get any bills. Also my number must be wrong because it's not coming up under my account. And also why I can't log in because non of the information they put in for me was correct. Apparently this guy can't even change anything in his system. So I still can't pay my bill, can't log in because numbers not tied to it. I thought ATT was bad and is known as being bad. I was really looking forward to getting rid of my ATT but I don't think you can top the bad experience I've had with Tmobile. I will never recommend them to anyone and complete waste of 150 bucks so far. I was hopeful and was going to switch cable, internet, phone, cell phones to them but they really dropped the ball on my account in every way it appears.

Would give 0 stars but that's not an option. By far the worst phone service provider on the market. Customer service is a joke and phone coverage is pathetic. Live in Atlanta and get absolutely 0 cell service. And not to mention the price. Almost double the cost of other carriers. Phone prices are shy high to boot. Need to take a loan just to buy a phone. I'm so glad i switched to a different carrier

One star because I can't rate any lower. Was a sprint customer for many years and only had minor inconvenient issues. Now, nothing but problems. Horrible customer service, if you can even call it that. Our phones stopped receiving calls from land line numbers. After an hour on the phone with some boob, mine is fine, another hour and a half on the phone with 3 different boobs, and more than a week later, my husband's still cannot receive incoming calls from land lines. This would include work calls. I am livid. I will pay off this new phone and take it ANYWHERE else. Oh, and as I am writing this review I am on hold yet again about the same issue. Nearly 30 minutes and no human voice except the same adds.

Listen to the masses of 1 star reviews here, I had given T Mobile 5 stars in the past. I can no longer in good faith do that ever again. I have been deceived, shrugged off, out and out sold something that was not true to form and to top that off, when it came time for them to make it right, I was told that the official reason for the mistake that THEY made was buyers remorse! LOL LOL LOL! nooo. The only remorse I have is that T Mobile is now the worst network provider in all of the land. I guess when you buy a huge false ad campaign claiming you are the largest network leader, upping prices, hidden costs and selling lies to the customer is how you pay for it. If any of you feel like they stole from you as I did, use FairShake.com and get your money back. It was the only way they would listen to me.
Sherrelle at South boulevard office in Charlotte provided me with outstanding service today.  She was knowledgeable pleasant and resolved my problem quickly
disrespectful, unhelpful, incompetent, worst conversation i’ve ever had with a customer service representative. t mobile is a terrible company

I don't know what happened to T-mobile. I've had them since 2008 and just suddenly in this past year their service has gotten so terrible.
I drove across Iowa, Nebraska, Wyoming, and had virtually no service the entire time, on the main highway. I get it's rural but that's a 3rd of the country without service. 
I live in Utah, and used to be fine at least here. Now, driving from Salt lake to Ogden, I was on a work call and had to call back 7 times. Same thing if I try and call my wife. This is an extremely populated area, not rural, and I consistently get my call dropped the entire 45 mile stretch. This is almost unfunctionable.

Beware of products that T-Mobile adds to your service agreement because once you sign it you must pay. I contacted T-Mobile via their online Chat app to transfer two Cell lines from Verizon.  This took a very long time and near the end the Rep, Chaz Hill, mentioned adding a LG Gpad5, as if it was free. I said OK. Weeks later I found out that I had to pay for the device and the service.  I then cancelled the service but was stuck with a $300 bill for a product that I had no intention of ordering. I feel that this is a deceptive selling practice.
After T-Mobile took over Sprint (was with Sprint for 15 years), it has been garbage service. I am switching to Verizon immediately. If I could give negative stars, I would.

Please be aware if you go to get T-Mobile you will not be told the truth about any of their service. Then when you cancel your service and have paid Iphone's and other phones off they don't want to unlock themselves as carriers as by rights I have talked to Apple they said as long as phones are paid off that whatever company it is does have to unlock the carrier. Not only all of that they had me send a phone I owed on to them that they were suppose to payoff and never did I had to pay it off plus they kept my phone and resold it so yet again a win win for T-Mobile to mess with the little man. I personal would advise no one to do business with T-Moble. Please take note you have been told about their nonsense practices.

I had an excellent experience with an employee named Dawn Zeman at the Oconomowoc Store.  She was very patient and helpful, aiding me through a situation where other employees weren't helpful, and didn't want to help me out.

The worst company ever!!!! 
I do not recommend T-mobile!!!

I have been with them for 20 years!!
Their data services are low quality, call dropped on different areas, sales branches are thieves and sign you up with crab, costumer satisfaction level low mostly overseas call center, 5G is BS.
They just don't have it.
I am thinking already for long time to switch to Verizon or any better.
Att is another BS.
We truly don't have a good carrier in the US the only one who I want to try is Verizon.
They are more pricey but I will finally switch soon.

I live in the town center of Reston, VA, in the Washington DC metro area. I use T-mobile prepaid.

I have complained several times to T-mobile that I receive little (one signal bar) or no reception at my location. Each time I call their call center, the customer executives perform routine rigmarole of asking to confirm my identity, questions about my device and operating system etc. Each time I tell them, it is the latest IPhone running latest version of iOS. Next they ask a dozen useless questions about which call got dropped, specify the number your were on the call with, the time for the call etc. After wasting another ten or fifteen minutes, they finally  acknowledge that it is a known issue by admitting “we are aware of a technical issue in your area and our engineering team is working to address the issue”.


This issue and the above exchange with t mobile call center has been going on for two months now.

When I request an estimate for the timeline of resolution of this known technical issue, they say they can’t provide that. Then they add the hollow and meaningless “we are focused on providing great customer experience....” Mumbo-jumbo. And they issue an account credit, as if bribing customers to stick around with their pathetic service.

Get your act together. Be transparent to customers. If you can’t resolve a known technical issue in your network in two months, just say that you can’t do it anytime soon. Don’t make hollow promises to resolve when you don’t have the intent to commit to a date.

Enable the customer to choose whether they wish to do business with you or not by admitting you don’t or can’t resolve the known technical issue. 


Don’t make empty promises of resolution without a firm commitment to do that by a date. You can say any date, however out in future, but specifying no date shows lack of commitment. Don’t keep your customers hanging in there, wondering what the hell happened.

Don’t test your customer’s patience

Most importantly don’t prevent your customers from being able to talk with family and friends uninterrupted without dropped calls due to your poor network.

If I could give t-mobile 0 stars I would. I have been a loyal customer for about 8 years. Back in October, I went in to get a new phone. They had a promotion going on "Buy one iPhone, get one free". The girl working there explained it to us and she got us to sign up for the promo. They told us that we were only going to be paying for one phone. However, I was being charged for both phones. I went in to solve the problem and it took them 3 months to fix it. They kept telling me to go back every month to see if the credit was being applied. I had to wait every single month to see if the credit was applied!!! Ridiculous! After, three months they decided to manually apply the credit. However, they only applied $125 instead of the full amount. So now I have to back and try to get them to fix it. They also have the worst signal ever! I have to pay for a phone that doesn't even work, I ALWAYS have to be connected to wifi.

6 years of service under T-Mobile in St.Lucie County Florida area. When we started good service 6 months later they dropped all the towers in our area multiple promises to get it better led to nothing but broken promises. My fault for putting up with it as long as I did. All over our county and surrounding areas drop calls and low low speed data if any. Done with T-Mobile.

There was a misunderstanding with my monthly bill.  I don't handle computers/online services all that well.
Nick in the Palm Harbor (FL) store was very kind and helped me resolve the issue to everyone's satisfaction. He needn't have done that: he did it because he cared, not because he was told to do so.   Again, my thanks.
